codeReview.summary: The implementation stores VF interferences and correctly enforces geometric tolerance, but its trimmed-face containment check is incorrect for faces with holes because it requires the projected point to be inside every boundary wire.

issues.description: `point_projects_inside_face` uses `face.boundaries().into_iter().all(...)`, which rejects every point on a trimmed face that contains inner loops. `Face::boundaries()` returns all oriented boundary wires, including holes, so a valid point must be inside the outer loop and outside inner loops rather than inside all loops. This breaks the required face-bounds check for any face with holes.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `rust-bop-worker` skill still requires `cargo clippy -p truck-bop` to pass even though mission guidance and `.factory/services.yaml` document that clippy is skipped because of known out-of-scope workspace failures.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/rust-bop-worker/SKILL.md:35-40 requires clippy cleanup, while `/Users/dongpengcheng/.factory/missions/28558208-113c-4faf-9654-e1aaded1d9ab/AGENTS.md` and `.factory/services.yaml` say clippy may fail or is skipped for this mission.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker had to infer trimmed-face containment semantics from `Face::boundaries()`, but that rule was not yet captured in shared library knowledge at the time of implementation.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: The transcript shows repeated inspection of `truck-topology/src/face.rs` before implementing containment, and `truck-topology/src/face.rs:101-106` shows `boundaries()` returns all oriented wires. This project knowledge is now recorded in `.factory/library/architecture.md`.

summary: Reviewed handoff, transcript skeleton, skill, and commit `e8a5890a`. The feature meets storage and tolerance requirements, but the bounds check is wrong for trimmed faces with holes, so the review fails with one blocking issue.
